chapters.title: You share a stake in getting underprivileged kids into school
chapters.text:
It does not matter whether you are a donor or you are actively involved with a nonprofit or an NGO. Whether you give a single coin or a six-figure check. One thing worth knowing is that it matters to find a way to help children from many poor countries get to school.

Education is the roadmap to a successful life, and every child has a right to a full life.

There is no amount too small or too big to give, because you are actively contributing to the development of society.

As long as you can dream it, you can achieve it, and that is what Adam Braun talks about in this book. There is no place too small to find inspiration, and as long as you are dedicated to reaching your goals, you can make a difference.

An item as small as a pencil can be deeply symbolic, because some children around the world cannot even afford one. The promise of a pencil can go a long way in shaping the life of a child and many others, as this summary explains. You can always help people, and if you are looking for ways to start and how to go about it, this summary comes highly recommended.

Read it till the end.

chapters.title: Turning that first spark into real steps is essential for success
chapters.text:
Ervin Braun, Adam Braun's father, was an intimidating man who expected his children to give their best effort no matter the circumstances. Adam and his siblings, a brother and a sister, were to follow their father's rules at all times. Their father's preferences guided their movements, and they dared not disappoint him. That upbringing taught Adam a great deal, and he understood that, strict as his father was, he was only trying to enforce discipline.

Ervin Braun understood the value of education, which is why he wanted his children to be the best versions of themselves. It is this belief that carried Adam Braun across the world.

The value of education cannot be overstated, because it is the lens through which the world can be seen.

Education is not limited to the four walls of a classroom. Although Adam played basketball in high school and dreamed of going pro, he changed his mind. In his sophomore year of college, he decided to go on a spiritual quest. He signed up with a group of travelers on an educational program and began his trip across the world on a ship. The ship ran into trouble at sea, taking on serious damage. Adam prayed and felt a spiritual presence inside him that assured him he would survive.

A sense of the spiritual can give you extra motivation if you believe it is there.

In every country they visited, Adam asked children what they would want most if they could have anything in the world. The children gave all sorts of answers, but Adam was struck by a boy who said he wanted a pencil. He gave the boy a pencil and concluded that every pencil carries a purpose. To that boy, a pencil was all he needed to pursue what he really wanted. The pencil stood for a way to get an education, and this deepened Adam's belief that every child deserves quality schooling.

Read it till the end.

chapters.title: Building a quality, sustainable education system is a shared responsibility
chapters.text:
Adam Braun came up with the idea to start a nonprofit that provides basic quality schooling at the primary level for children from poor or developing countries. He named the organization Pencils of Promise, or PoP. In his first three months, Adam raised more than $35,000 in donations.

His first school, set up in Pha Theung in Laos, was a huge success. Almost the whole village turned up for the groundbreaking ceremony. The people were thrilled to finally have a school, giving Adam indescribable joy.

Schooling is central to the growth of any society. Without it, ignorance takes over and leads to regression and stagnation.

Using social media, Adam launched a platform where people could see his work and read his mission statements. He partnered with strong brands and discovered that people were ready to help when it came to raising money for schooling. He also mentored young people and gave them a reason to advocate for education for children in deprived areas.

Adam believes schooling plays a defining role in a child's life and in the kind of adult that child will become. As he continued to build schools, he received a great deal of help from people and whole communities. Some pledged manual labor, while others gave monetary donations or land.

Setting up a learning facility is a shared responsibility, both within and outside a community. The benefits of a strong educational system are not limited to one person. They serve the general interest of the whole society.

Adam's organization gained wider exposure when he met his brother's friend. The friend, Justin Bieber, was a budding musician whose fan base was growing every day. Bieber became a committed member of PoP and used his youthful popularity to spread its message to more people around the world.

Schooling opens the mind to many things and widens your horizon beyond what sits in front of you. There is no limit to what you can achieve as long as you are dedicated to it. Small steps grow into big projects when the cause is just.

chapters.title: A realized goal is only real when a plan turns the idea into action
chapters.text:
Adam Braun made a plan to officially launch the PoP story in the media and show people what they were about. To do that, he needed to expand and give the organization a serious look.

When priorities clash in those moments, stay guided by your values, not by what feels urgent, as Adam Braun has written.

Almost two years after launch, he hired paid staff and moved to a bigger office. He brought in stronger professionals and set up a team of strong volunteers. Adam built his big announcements on three main frameworks. The amazing story framework aimed to create a moment where people's first reaction to seeing the mission statement would be awe mixed with a small pang of regret, since they would be annoyed at themselves for not hearing about PoP sooner. He wanted a story so compelling that people would be eager to be part of the projects. The online presence framework gave people a chance to watch the organization's progress and offer feedback. This would let the organization know how people reacted and welcome their suggestions and ideas. Adam saw the online presence as a key factor for funds, donations, and referrals. The professional organization framework was based on the idea that people's perception of an organization rests on its professional look and reliability. With a bigger space and more staff, PoP could take on bigger projects and partner with stronger brands.

A plan on its own is not enough to start a project. You have to take concrete steps that convince people to buy into your ideas.

PoP received nationwide recognition when HuffPost ran a cover story on its mission and intentions. Many organizations and news outlets requested interviews and a chance to support the organization's growth. Major TV networks aired those interviews, giving PoP a wider audience. Justin Bieber also donated a substantial amount of money, which let Adam and his team build more schools.

chapters.title: A successful organization builds happiness, friendship, reward, improvement, and fun
chapters.text:
Adam Braun's journey with PoP gave him the idea for the five personalities that defined his organization. These personalities became the principles of the organization.

Any organization that wants to build a healthy and sustainable work environment should adopt these five principles: Happiness, Friendship, Reward, Improvement, and Fun.

When your employees are happy coming to work, you have built an environment where productivity will always rise.

When people feel satisfied with what they do, they are bonded by the success of a well-executed project. Helping children from poor countries get basic quality schooling is bound to spark happiness and create a friendship bond. Braun set up organizational events such as meditation breaks and jukebox sessions, where employees take turns playing their favorite songs.

As a way to appreciate donors and supporters, Adam also introduced a Closing the Loop program. This program assembles videos, audio, and pictures into a documentary that says thank you to donors and supporters and shows them what their contributions have achieved. It acts as a reward system, so donors and supporters feel they are part of something big and meaningful.

In moments of uncertainty, immerse yourself in the life you would be most proud to live, as Adam Braun has written.

Using feedback, Adam also created a way for people to share suggestions through a Launch and Learn program. This program fueled the organization's growth as people came in with useful ideas. He also borrowed from his father's principles about giving your best effort. Every employee at PoP knew they had to be at their best all the time. Volunteers who were not contributing were phased out, and more people were brought in to become active contributors.

An organization's success rests on the ability to improve by learning and reacting.

Finally, Adam made sure people had fun working with him and the organization. People were free to contribute and share ideas, and he built his organization on a foundation of friendliness and service.

chapters.title: It is fine to be scared by the size of a project, but never let that scare stop you
chapters.text:
As the years went by, PoP grew bigger and stronger, which meant Adam had to expand. The size of the projects ahead of him was intimidating, but instead of giving up, he decided to press onward.

First, he met with Ray Chambers, a renowned philanthropist with years of experience. Chambers was interested in his ideas and joined the board. He taught Braun the value of spirituality in relation to service and urged him not to overlook the spiritual side of his work.

Second, Braun enrolled in a program at Harvard on Exponential Fundraising. The program taught him how to ask for donations without feeling awkward and how to convince donors to part with large sums of money. When he met Paul Foster, an investment banker, Braun put his training to work and asked for a six-figure donation. To his delight, he got it, along with a useful piece of advice. Foster told him that if he wanted to collect large sums, he had to look the part, dress the part, and be convincing. He also told Braun to follow up by constantly sharing what he did with the money. The advice paid off, and he was able to raise a great deal of money for the organization.

The first step to tackling a problem is to build a conviction inside yourself that you will find a way around it.

You must never be put off by a project simply because you think it is too big. It is never too late to admit that you need more knowledge. Go ahead and gain it, because no one has a monopoly on knowledge.

Adam's PoP organization had built over 200 schools by 2014, with plans to build at least 300 more. The team had also hired more people and was planning to train over a thousand teachers worldwide.

To give more to the community, they set up a scholarship program aimed at supporting at least 10,000 students by the end of 2015. The scale of these plans shows how far a single step and the determination to succeed can take you.

For context, according to United Institute of Statistics data, almost 60 percent of youth between the ages of about 15 and 17 in Africa are not in school.

chapters.title: Closing thought
chapters.text:
The decisions you make can shape the lives of people who live thousands of miles away. Your dreams are valid as long as you are determined to see them through. Living your dream requires sacrifice and a commitment to learn and unlearn many things. You cannot know it all, which is why you have to stay open-minded.

Adam Braun's decision came from the inspiration he found in a child who needed a pencil. This shows that little things inspire some of the biggest dreams. You do not have to wait for a big break before you start achieving your goals. Small steps will take you to greater heights if you stay focused and dedicated. The best thing you can do for yourself is believe in yourself. Self-belief gives you the confidence to navigate storms and come out on top.

Education is essential and mandatory. It is the vehicle that carries you across the world from where you stand. It lets you travel across miles without leaving the spot where you are. Schooling gives you more significant opportunities and helps you expand beyond what is in front of you. You are never too small or too old to dream of doing something great. Like Adam Braun, you can draw inspiration from small things around you, develop your immediate environment, and affect the wider world.

Try this. Meet a child today and share the value of education. Do what you can to raise funds for the less privileged around you so they can get a quality education.

Read it till the end.
